title: Fix return type of bytes.join()
body:
This was already tested in `tests/run/bytesmethods.pyx`, but there were apparently (non-trivial?) cases where the return type wasn't correctly inferred.

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Update the builtin declaration for bytes.join in Cython/Compiler/Builtin.py to return 'T' (the receiver type) instead of 'O', so the compiler correctly infers that bytes.join returns a bytes object. Validate by running the bytes-related tests (e.g., tests/run/bytesmethods.pyx) and the full test suite.

steps.operation: Change bytes.join builtin return type from generic object to receiver type
steps.reason: The builtin_types_table currently declares bytes.join with return type 'O' (generic PyObject), preventing the compiler from inferring a bytes return in some call sites. Aligning bytes.join with str.join by declaring its return type as 'T' (the same type as the receiver) enables the type inferencer to produce a 'bytes' type for bytes.join results.
steps.side_effects: Type inference will now treat bytes.join(...) as returning bytes across the codebase, which should fix tests expecting a bytes return (notably tests/run/bytesmethods.pyx). Downstream code that relied on the previous generic PyObject return type shouldn't be affected at runtime (no public API change), but some internal compiler codepaths and generated C may differ; this could expose other places where bytearray.join inference is lacking (bytearray currently has no explicit builtin entry). Run full test suite; if any tests or downstream modules depended on the prior generic typing (unlikely), they may need minor adjustments. No server/runtime compatibility impact, but CI and users rebuilding Cython modules may produce different, but semantically equivalent, generated C.
